af_score_components.notes: Agent Observability MCP. Has MCP (quality 75). Monitoring/observability for agents. Documentation good. Auth for observability backends. Rate limits for metrics/logs. DevOps focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Auth for observability platforms (Datadog, New Relic, etc.). Logs/metrics could contain PII. Fine-grained access control. Secret redaction in logs critical. Audit trail for compliance.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Observability platform reliability (usually high). No SLA for MCP wrapper. Metric/log delivery should be fire-and-forget. Suitable for production agent monitoring.

af_score_components.notes: agent-pay — payment processing for agents. No MCP documented. Documentation sparse. Purpose for financial transactions. Highly sensitive (PCI compliance, fraud).

security_score_components.security_notes: Payment systems require highest security. HTTPS/TLS required. PCI-DSS compliance mandatory. Strong auth and authorization. No plaintext credentials. Tokenization/vault pattern essential. Fraud detection critical.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Payment system reliability critical. Idempotency essential (prevent double-charges). Transaction logging/auditing. Settlement timing. Not suitable for production without PCI certification.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Payment MCP. Has MCP (quality 75). Payment operations for agents. Documentation adequate. Auth for payment platforms. Rate limits for transactions. Financial operations focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS required. Payment platform auth (strong). PCI compliance if handling cards. No plaintext credentials. Encryption in transit/at rest. Audit logging for transactions. Fraud monitoring.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Payment reliability critical. Idempotency for retry safety. Transaction confirmation required. Settlement processing async. Suitable for automated payment workflows with proper governance.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Security Scanner MCP. Has MCP (quality 80). Security scanning for code/systems. Documentation excellent. Auth for scanner platforms. Rate limits for scans. Security operations focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Auth for scanning platforms. Access to source code (sensitive). Findings contain vulnerability details (disclosure risks). Fine-grained scoping (what to scan). Secret detection in code (must not log findings).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Scanner reliability important (security gates). Async scanning for large repos. Rate limits for comprehensive scans. Suitable for continuous security monitoring.

af_score_components.notes: Clalit Pharmacy Search agent skill. Has MCP (quality 60). Israeli health provider (Clalit) pharmacy integration. Documentation limited (Hebrew-focused). Healthcare sensitive. Regulatory compliance (HIPAA/local laws).

security_score_components.security_notes: Healthcare data (PII, medication history) highly sensitive. HTTPS enforced. Strong auth required. HIPAA/GDPR compliance. Patient data must be protected. Audit logging for compliance. Provider infrastructure security (Clalit).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Healthcare system reliability critical. Clalit infrastructure provides baseline. Medication availability queries time-sensitive. Integration timeliness important.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Skill Loader MCP. Has MCP (quality 70). Dynamic agent skill/capability loading. Documentation adequate. No built-in auth (agent responsibility). Rate limits for skill discovery. Framework focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: Dynamic loading risks (arbitrary code execution). Skill source validation critical. No auth in MCP (agent must validate sources). Sandboxing important. Trusted skill repositories only.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Skill loader reliability depends on skill repository. Broken skills could crash agents. Fallback handling essential. No SLA. Suitable for agent capability expansion with validation.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Skills MCP. Has MCP (quality 75). Skill/capability framework for agents. Documentation good. Auth depends on skill implementation. Rate limits for skill operations. Capability management focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: Skills can request elevated privileges. Auth/authz critical for capability access. Principle of least privilege. Credential scoping per skill. No over-privileged agents.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Skill framework reliability (middleware). Individual skill failures isolated. Fallback to alternative skills. Suitable for composable agent architectures.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Skills Authoring MCP. Has MCP (quality 70). Tools for writing agent skills. Documentation adequate. No auth required (development tool). Rate limits for compilation/testing. Developer tools focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: Dev tool security depends on usage. Can expose secret patterns in example code. Testing tools might log sensitive data. Developer responsibility for secret handling.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Dev tool reliability (build/test performance). No SLA. Suitable for skill development workflows.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Toolkit MCP. Has MCP (quality 75). Utility functions for agents. Documentation good. No auth required (utilities). Rate limits not applicable. General-purpose utilities.

security_score_components.security_notes: Utilities could have unintended side effects. Input validation important (prevent injection). No inherent privilege escalation. Good code review needed.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Utility library reliability. Well-tested utilities. No SLA. Suitable for agent development workflows.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Trust MCP. Has MCP (quality 75). Trust/reputation framework for agents. Documentation adequate. Auth for trust operations. Rate limits for trust queries. Trust verification focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: Trust/identity systems require strong security. HTTPS enforced. Auth for trust operations. Prevents spoofing/impersonation. Reputation scoring (prevents Sybil attacks). Audit trail for trust decisions.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Trust system availability critical. Reputation data consistency. No SLA. Suitable for multi-agent systems requiring trust verification.
